prompt: Implement a Community Fulfillment & Submission Operations Console for school districts and neighborhood group-leader programs that must run fully offline as a responsive React single-page application. The system supports Student and Teacher request submissions that move through a draft -> submit -> review -> return -> archive workflow, where Reviewers can add timestamped comments, attach exception reasons, and trigger a mandatory secondary review when key fields (requesting org/class, item SKU, quantity, or delivery window) change after initial approval. Operations users manage fulfillment through a unified search experience that combines multi-criteria filters (item/SKU, lot, warehouse location, document status, requester, date range) with sortable, paginated tables, saved filter presets, and fast actions from a drawer (split shipment into multiple packages, assign carrier name and tracking number, record delivery confirmation, or log exceptions like "damaged" or "recipient unavailable"). Barcode scan is supported for rapid locating and actions using an on-device camera scanner component, with a manual fallback entry and configurable check digit validation. Community Admins can maintain service areas and bind group leaders to locations, define commission rules (e.g., 3.5% of attributed order value, rounded to the nearest cent), and configure settlement cycles (e.g., weekly on Fridays at 6:00 PM) along with performance attribution rules when multiple leaders overlap a service area. All data processing and persistence must be browser-only, using IndexedDB as the primary database, LocalStorage for lightweight preferences (UI state, last-used filters), and local file import/export for portability. Bulk import/export uses CSV/JSON templates generated in-app via Blob/FileSaver; imports run client-side template validation (required columns, data types, max 5,000 rows per file), show row-level error details, and apply an all-or-nothing rollback if any row fails validation. A plugin architecture enables offline extension through locally packaged adapters/parsers/cleaners and storage backends discovered at runtime from a local JSON manifest; each plugin implements unified interfaces for reading source files, normalizing fields, and writing to IndexedDB. Access control is enforced via RBAC with role and resource-level permissions (Student, Teacher, Warehouse Staff, Operations, Finance, Admin), plus org/class-scope authorization; every create/update/approve/ship action writes a tamper-evident audit record (hash-chained) and masks sensitive fields in UI (e.g., partial addresses). Authentication is local username + password only, with passwords hashed via Web Crypto PBKDF2 and an auto-lock after 15 minutes idle. A message center provides in-app notifications only (no email/SMS/push), with templates, variables, subscription preferences, deduplication within 60 seconds, priority queues, and delivery receipts recorded locally. Although the long-term target stack is Express + React + MongoDB, this offline deliverable emulates Express-style service endpoints and Mongo-like collections entirely in the frontend service layer over IndexedDB to remain fully functional without any network dependency.
